questions.questionText: Under the Mental Healthcare Act 2017 (India), a mentally ill person has the right to:
questions.optionB: Advance directive — a written document specifying how the person wants to be treated for mental illness in a future episode when they may lack decision-making capacity
questions.explanation: Core concept: MHCA 2017 provides advance directive right to mental health patients. Why correct: Key provisions: (1) Advance directive — right to specify treatment preferences. (2) Nominated representative. (3) Right to community living. (4) Suicide attempt decriminalized (Section 115 — person shall be presumed to have severe stress, NOT tried under IPC 309). (5) Right to access mental healthcare. (6) No electroconvulsive therapy without anesthesia for adults; banned for <18 years. Why A wrong: MHCA restricts involuntary admission (needs psychiatrist certification, review board). Why C wrong: Right to informed consent and right to refuse specific treatments. Why D wrong: Presumption of legal capacity. Clinical relevance: Replaced Mental Health Act 1987. Progressive legislation with rights-based approach. Exam trap: MHCA 2017 DECRIMINALIZED suicide attempt — person not punished but offered care and support. This is a frequently tested point. Memory aid: 'MHCA 2017 = Mental health with rights + Advance directive + suicide decriminalized.' Reference: Ahuja – Ethics and Law.
questions.conceptTag: MHCA 2017 advance directive suicide decriminalized rights
